info.title: AutoShares Trading API
info.description: AutoShares Trading API — trade equities, options, and ETFs. Manage orders, positions, watchlists, price alerts, and streaming market data.

get.description: This endpoint enables you to retrieve information about a specific ACH relationship of some trading account.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: ID of the trading account whose ACH relationship must be retrieved.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the ACH relationship whose information must be retrieved.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: This is the version of the API. Unless you have multiple versions of AutoShares Trader’s API deployed in your environment, leave it at 1.0.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: This is the authorization token that you retrieved from the first endpoint (/token).

parameters.name: Et-App-Key
parameters.description: This is your app’s unique key that can be retrieved from the BO Companies widget in AutoShares Trader.

200.description: Successful request, JSON object containing information about the ACH relationship is returned.

401.description: The access level of the provided authorization token is not sufficient to perform this operation.

422.description: A validation error occurred while processing the request.

get.description: Validates a third-party app's authorization request and returns metadata needed to render the consent screen. The user's browser is redirected here from the third-party app.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: The third-party app's client_id, issued by the Developer Portal.

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: Where to send the user after they grant or deny consent. Must match exactly a URI registered for the app.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Space-separated list of requested scopes (e.g. "read orders"). Must be a subset of the app's registered scopes.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Random opaque string the third-party app generates. Echoed back to defeat CSRF.

parameters.name: response_type
parameters.description: Currently only "code" is supported.

200.description: Authorization request validated. Body contains metadata for the consent screen.

400.description: redirect_uri not registered, or scope exceeds app permissions.

404.description: Unknown client_id, or app has been revoked.

post.description: Back-channel endpoint. Third-party app's server (never browser) exchanges a single-use authorization code for an access token bound to the user who granted consent.

grant_type.description: Must be "authorization_code". The refresh_token grant is queued for a future release.

code.description: The authorization code received at the redirect_uri.

client_secret.description: The third-party app's confidential client secret. Server-side only.

200.description: Access token issued.

access_token.description: Bearer token for all /api/v1/* calls. Prefix: "asat_".

400.description: Invalid grant_type, code expired/used, or client_id mismatch.

401.description: Invalid client_secret.

post.description: Kills a token before its TTL. RFC 7009 — always returns 200, even for unknown tokens, to prevent enumeration.

200.description: Always returned per RFC 7009.

get.description: Returns the AutoShares user whose access token was used to authenticate, plus the third-party app's identity and the granted scopes.

200.description: OK

userId.description: AutoShares user ID (Entra OIDC sub claim).

appName.description: Display name of the third-party app.

grantedScopes.description: Scopes the user granted on this token.

enabledScopes.description: Scopes the user has enabled at the platform level. Per-user ceiling.

accountCount.description: Number of AutoShares accounts the user owns.

401.description: Missing or invalid Authorization header.

get.description: Returns every account the authenticated user owns. The third-party app can only act on accounts in this list — passing any other account number to subsequent endpoints results in 403.

200.description: OK

401.description: Missing or invalid Authorization header.

get.description: Returns current balances for one account the user owns. AutoShares verifies the account is owned by the authenticated user before forwarding to AutoShares.

parameters.name: acct_no
parameters.description: Clearing account number from /api/v1/accounts.

200.description: OK

401.description: Missing or invalid Authorization header.

403.description: Account does not belong to the authenticated user.

post.description: Submits an order on behalf of the authenticated user. Requires the "orders" scope on the access token AND the user must have "orders" in their enabled scopes.

parameters.name: acct_no
parameters.description: Clearing account number.

reference.description: Free-form tag stamped on the order for /pnl source attribution.

200.description: Order submitted to upstream broker.

401.description: Missing or invalid Authorization header.

403.description: Token missing required scope "orders", user disabled "orders" scope, or account does not belong to user.

get.description: Returns every developer app owned by the authenticated AutoShares user. The client_secret is NEVER returned here — only the public client_id.

200.description: OK

401.description: Not signed in.

post.description: Creates a new developer app and returns the client_secret EXACTLY ONCE. The secret is sha256-hashed at rest and cannot be retrieved again.

name.description: Display name shown on the consent screen.

redirectUris.description: Where AutoShares sends users after consent. Each URI must match exactly.

scopes.description: Scopes the app can request. The user can authorize fewer but never more.

clientSecret.description: RETURNED ONCE — never again. Store in server-side env config.

400.description: Missing name or redirectUris.

401.description: Not signed in.

delete.description: Marks the app as revoked and kills every outstanding access token issued for it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: App ID (from POST /api/dev-portal/apps).

200.description: App revoked.

401.description: Not signed in.

404.description: App not found or owned by another user.

get.description: Returns per-app, per-day API call counts for every app owned by the authenticated user. Drives the usage graph in the Developer Portal.

200.description: OK

401.description: Not signed in.

Id.description: Unique order id. Assigned by OMS.

Price.description: Price (not used for some order types). Assigned by client.

StopPrice.description: Stop price (used for stop orders). Assigned by client.

ClientId.description: Client order id (ClOrdID). Assigned by OMS.

ParentClientId.description: Client order id (ClOrdID) of parent order in a case of mleg order.

OrigClientId.description: Client order id (ClOrdID). Assigned by OMS.

LastQuantity.description: Last transaction executed quantity. Assigned by executor.

AveragePrice.description: Average execution price. Assigned by executor.

Date.description: Last modification date. Assigned by OMS.

TransactionDate.description: Last transaction date. Assigned by OMS or executor.

SettDate.description: date of settlement. Received from execution venue or calculated by OMS, assigned by OMS or executor.

Status.description: Current order status. Assigned by OMS or executor.

ExecutionStatus.description: Last modification status. Assigned by OMS or executor.

RequestStatus.description: Indicate order processing status within OMS. Assigned by OMS.

Description.description: Used to indicate reject reason or broker comment. Assigned by executor.

Comment.description: Client side comments. Assigned by client.

TimeInForce.description: Time in force. Assigned by client.

ExecInstNum.description: Number Execution instruction. Assigned by client.

CounterPartyOrderId.description: Executor order id. Assigned by executor.

ParentId.description: Parent order id. Assigned by OMS.

Legs.description: Multileg order legs. Assigned by client.

ExecutionVenue.description: Executor name, to which the order was routed. Assigned by OMS router.

ExtendedHours.description: Indicates the extended trading session in which the order is eligible to execute. Wire values are lowercase-hyphen strings (NOT the title-case labels surfaced in AutoShares Trader's dropdown). Valid values: "Regular", "pre-market", "post-market", "All sessions". Omit (or set to "Regular") for regular-session-only orders. TimeInForce="Day" is valid for ext-hours; orders go live in the current session - no need for GoodTillCrossing. Verified 2026-05-22.

IsExternal.description:
This property indicates that order was accepted from external system and fields like ClientOrderId should be persisted.
It's a hack for orders from MessageAcceptor.
Assigned be client.

ExecBrocker.description: Order route name. Assigned by executor.
